differential_diagnoses: Dabrafenib and trametinib-induced systemic adverse reaction (pyrexia/hepatotoxicity)
Autoimmune hepatitis or immune-related adverse event (irAE)
Systemic metastatic melanoma with paraneoplastic symptoms
Occult biliary pathology (e.g., choledocholithiasis not seen on ultrasound)
next_steps: Permanent discontinuation of dabrafenib and trametinib
Peripheral blood smear for morulae (to rule out anaplasmosis)
Serologic testing for Anaplasma phagocytophilum and Ehrlichia chaffeensis
Serum ferritin, soluble CD25, and triglycerides to evaluate for HLH
MRI/MRCP of the abdomen to evaluate for biliary duct pathology
Consider liver biopsy if liver function tests continue to trend upward
Autoimmune hepatitis serologic panel (ANA, ASMA, IgG levels)
